summary: PR #2271 matched 11 item-system functions across Lugia, Oldkuri, Whitebea, Nessbat, Ness PK Thunder Ball, Matadogas, Hitodeman, Great Fox Laser, and Kirby item files. It also refined shared item attribute/item-var structs and replaced two UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS header prototypes with concrete signatures. The PR body claims all 11 functions are 100% matched, and the bot report confirms 11 new 100% matches plus a small .sdata2 improvement in ithitodeman.

key_files.role: Shared item attribute/item-var struct updates for Lugia, Hitodeman, and Whitebea fields used by matched functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itLugiaAttributes adds x8/xC/x10; itHitodeman_ItemVars adds x84; itWhiteBeaAttributes adds pad_x8 and x14; itLugia_ItemVars adds x60

evidence_refs: raw_diff: UNK_RET it_802D4494(UNK_PARAMS) changed to void it_802D4494(Item_GObj* gobj)

what_changed: Replaced 11 decomp placeholder comments with C function bodies in item source files.

Updated shared item structs in itCommonItems.h so matched functions can access concrete fields such as itLugiaAttributes.x10, itLugia_ItemVars.x60, itHitodeman_ItemVars.x84, and itWhiteBeaAttributes.x14.
Recovered header signatures for ithitodeman.h: it_802D4494(Item_GObj* gobj), and itlugia.h: it_802D1830(Item_GObj* gobj).
Added item-related includes to itgreatfoxlaser.c and itkirby_2F23.c where the new bodies call item helpers or use item types.
Added a local forward declaration for grIceMt_801FA6D8 in itwhitebea.c.
Bot report recorded 11 new 100% matches totaling +1336 matched bytes and one .sdata2 improvement in ithitodeman.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired matched bodies with the struct field additions needed to express item attributes and item vars as typed accesses instead of leaving all accessed offsets hidden in padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys reads attrs->x10 and itemVar.lugia.x60

smart_moves.text: Header prototypes were updated when bodies established concrete signatures, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS surface.
The PR kept many globally unknown address-style function names such as it_802D23F4 and it_802AB3F0 rather than inventing semantics not shown by the slice.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item matches, recovering small attribute/item-var fields in shared structs can be necessary to make short functions match with typed field accesses.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new item function bodies consume those fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched body proves a function signature, update the owning header from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the concrete signature in the same change.

Several matched item setup functions share a pattern of setting life timer or animation state, assigning hitlag callbacks, and calling common item helper routines; comparing sibling item setup functions may be useful for future matches.

naming_conventions.convention: Use short local role names like gobj, ip, and attrs for item GObj, Item*, and attribute pointer locals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated Item* ip and attrs locals in matched functions

naming_conventions.convention: Keep address-style names for still-unknown functions when the PR evidence does not prove a semantic name.
evidence_refs: PR body summary includes it_802D23F4, it_802AB3F0, it_802CB798, it_802D4494, it_802EB1EC, it_802F2810
raw_diff function definitions retain address-style names

naming_conventions.convention: Use item-specific union arms for item vars, such as ip->xDD4_itemVar.lugia, .hitodeman, .whitebea, .greatfoxlaser, and .pkthunder when the item context supports them.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itemVar union arm accesses across Lugia, Hitodeman, Whitebea, Great Fox Laser, PK Thunder Ball

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used narrowly in several short matched item functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The slice shows bot match evidence, but no review discussion explaining why each PAD_STACK was required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use post-decrement in a countdown condition for Lugia phys: if (ip->xDD4_itemVar.lugia.x60-- != 0).
Use a bitwise OR chain over itcmd vars to choose between two Item_80268E5C modes.
For nullable output helpers, preserve early NULL-return structure and explicit Vec3 zeroing fallback.
A raw byte-pointer stride cast remains in it_802AB3F0: ip = (Item*) ((u8*) ip + idx * 0x18).
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This matches the slice but remains a typed-fields/pointer-math follow-up candidate if a real element type is later recovered.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Matched code increased to 56.03% (+0.03%, +1336 bytes), with 11 new matches all moving from 0.00% to 100.00%; also reported .sdata2 improvement in main/melee/it/items/ithitodeman from 37.50% to 57.89%.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching it_802AB3F0 or pkthunder xDEC to see whether the raw idx * 0x18 pointer stride gained a real type.
Search later changes to itWhitebea.c for grIceMt_801FA6D8 declaration ownership or header placement.
Search later PRs replacing PAD_STACK in item functions matched here.
Search for sibling item setup functions using xD44_lifeTimer, Item_80268E5C, efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, and it_8026B3A8.
Search itCommonItems.h evolution for semantic names replacing x14/x18/x84/x60 in Lugia, Hitodeman, and Whitebea structs.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2271 merged metadata and changed-file counts
human_pr_text.md: PR summary and all 100% matched claim
raw_diff: complete diff for 12 changed files
notes: There are no human review comments in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent and review-requested changes are unavailable.
The bot report supports 100% match status for the 11 named functions, but the slice does not include compiler commands, objdiff artifacts, or local build logs beyond the bot summary.
PAD_STACK uses have match evidence through the bot report, but the slice does not explain why each stack pad was necessary.
The raw PR JSON is truncated, but the loaded human text, changed-files list, raw diff, and text corpus are sufficient for this intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Item matches may require shared struct field recovery
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2271, several 100% item function matches were landed together with itCommonItems.h field additions for Lugia, Hitodeman, and Whitebea, enabling typed accesses in the recovered bodies.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover headers when bodies prove signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2271 updated ithitodeman.h and itlugia.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete Item_GObj* signatures where the implementation bodies made those signatures explicit.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow PAD_STACK use in matched item functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2271 used PAD_STACK(8) or PAD_STACK(16) in multiple short item functions that the bot report then listed as 100% matched; keep this as PR-specific evidence, not a general style preference.

source_update_candidates.title: it/items PR #2271 matched 11 item functions
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2271 is evidence that src/melee/it/items contains short item motion/setup/helper functions whose matches often pair common item helper call sequences with Item* local access, item-specific itemVar union arms, and shared attribute structs in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future path-scoped search and matching triage for item functions, but should be curated as a path fact rather than promoted directly from intake.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not generalize PAD_STACK from matched PRs without narrow evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2271 contains several PAD_STACK uses in functions reported as 100% matched. This supports retaining PAD_STACK only with narrow match evidence, not treating it as preferred style.
source_update_candidates.reason: This aligns with the existing matching-tactics standard; curator may decide whether this PR should be linked as an example rather than a new rule.

source_update_candidates.title: itwhitebea.c local ground callback declaration
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2271 added a local declaration for grIceMt_801FA6D8(void) in itwhitebea.c so it_802E4558 could call it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: void grIceMt_801FA6D8(void); and call in it_802E4558
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible path/include ownership follow-up; the slice does not prove whether a header should own this declaration.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the raw pointer stride in it_802AB3F0 as preferred style; the slice only shows it was part of a matched function, and typed recovery may still be possible later.
Do not infer semantic names for address-style functions from this PR; the author retained address-style names for several functions.
Do not claim human reviewer approval details or requested changes; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not claim that .sdata2 improvement was an intentional data-matching goal beyond the bot report noting it.
